info.title: OpenAI Chat API
info.description: OpenAI Chat Completion API including Image Analysis, Streaming, Function Calling, and Logprobs.

post.description: Generate a model response to a conversation. Beyond plain text, it supports image inputs in the message content, token-by-token streaming, function calling, and per-token log probabilities — letting one endpoint cover chat, multimodal analysis, and tool orchestration.

model.description: Identifier of the model to run the completion against. Picks the capability tier and pricing for the call; the available identifiers come from the model catalog rather than this field.

role.description: Who is speaking in this turn — typically the system (instructions), the user, or the assistant (the model's prior replies). It tells the model how to weigh the turn.

content.description: The payload of the turn. Pass a plain string for a text-only message, or an array of typed parts to mix text with image inputs for multimodal analysis.

type.description: Marks what kind of part this is within a multimodal content array, so each block is interpreted as text or as an image reference.

text.description: The text of a text-type content part.

image_url.description: Location of the image for an image-type content part; the model fetches and analyzes it alongside the text parts.

messages.description: The conversation so far, as an ordered list of turns. The model reads them in order and continues from the last one, so sequence carries the dialogue state.

stream.description: When on, the response is delivered as a stream of incremental chunks instead of one final payload — useful for showing output as it is generated. When off, the full reply returns in a single response.

logprobs.description: When on, the response includes the log probability of each returned token, letting you gauge the model's confidence at each step.

top_logprobs.description: How many of the most likely alternative tokens to report at each position, each with its log probability. Requires logprobs to be on.

name.description: The function's name, used by the model to refer to it when requesting a call and matched by your code when dispatching.

description.description: What the function does, written for the model. A clear description is the main signal the model uses to decide when this function fits the user's intent.

parameters.description: A JSON Schema describing the function's arguments. The model generates argument values that conform to it when it requests the call.

functions.description: Declares the functions the model may call. The model never runs them itself; it returns a structured request naming a function and its arguments, which your code then executes.

name.description: When forcing a call via the object form, the name of the function the model must invoke.

function_call.description: Controls function-calling behavior: pass a string mode such as `auto` to let the model decide whether to call, or an object naming one function to force that specific call.
